input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## Step 1 — Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Install the latest .NET SDK via winget
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## Step 2 — Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# Install MAUI (pulls in Android SDK, build tools, etc.)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## Step 3 — Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows target needs the Windows App SDK and WinUI. The simplest path:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or, if you only want build tools (no IDE):
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

Then add required components:

```powershell
# Modify the VS installation to include MAUI + UWP/WinUI components
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.WindowsAppSDK.Cs `
  --quiet --norestart
```

## Step 4 — Set Up the Android SDK

The MAUI workload installs an Android SDK, but you may need to accept licenses and discover what was installed:

```powershell
# Find where the Android SDK landed
$androidSdk = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA "Microsoft\Android\sdk"
# Or check the dotnet default:
$androidSdk = & dotnet --info | Select-String "Android" 

# List installed SDK platforms and build-tools
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Accept all licenses
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses

# Install a specific API level if needed (discover latest dynamically)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0" "platform-tools"
```

Set the environment variable so tools find it:

```powershell
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidSdk, "User")
```

## Step 5 — Set Up an Android Emulator

```powershell
# List available system images
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
  Select-String "system-images"

# Install an image (pick one from the list above)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create an AVD
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" `
  --device "pixel_6"

# Launch it
& "$androidSdk\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_MAUI
```

## Step 6 — Enable Windows Developer Mode

Required for deploying Windows (WinUI 3) apps:

```powershell
# Enable Developer Mode via registry
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /t REG_DWORD /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /d 1 /f
```

## Step 7 — Create & Run a Test Project

```powershell
# Scaffold a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Discover available target frameworks in the project
Select-String "TargetFrameworks" .\HelloMaui.csproj

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (must be running)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Install -t:Run
```

## Step 8 — Discover Versions Dynamically

```powershell
# Available .NET SDKs on your machine
dotnet --list-sdks

# Available workloads you can install
dotnet workload search

# Installed workloads + versions
dotnet workload list

# Installed Android SDK components
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list_installed

# Check which TFMs the new template uses (to match your SDK)
dotnet new maui --dry-run -n Probe 2>&1 | Select-String "net[0-9]"
```

---

**Key notes:**
- Replace `net9.0` with whatever version `dotnet --version` reports (e.g., `net10.0` if you install .NET 10).
- The `android-35` API level is current as of mid-2026; use `sdkmanager --list` to discover the latest.
- If Hyper-V isn't enabled (needed for fast Android emulation): `Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All`.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They can't run commands right now, so they want all the PowerShell commands laid out. Let me provide a comprehensive guide.
